Scenario: Verify QA user can start a new test session
Steps:
1. Log in as QA user
2. Go to Start Testing
3. Click Start New Session
ExpectedResult: New session created — test runner loads with all modules

Scenario: Verify new session is blocked if last session is still In Progress
Steps:
1. Start a session without completing it
2. Try to start another session
ExpectedResult: Error shown — must complete previous session first

Scenario: Verify session version number increments with each new session
Steps:
1. Complete a session
2. Start a new one
ExpectedResult: New session shows next version number (e.g. v2 after v1)

Scenario: Verify session shows correct start date and time
Steps:
1. Start a new session
2. Check session header
ExpectedResult: Start date and time shown correctly

Scenario: Verify Version Status can be updated to Completed
Steps:
1. Click Version Status button in test runner
2. Check module list
3. Select Completed
ExpectedResult: Session status updated to Completed — shown on session card

Scenario: Verify Version Status can be updated to Skipped
Steps:
1. Open Version Status modal
2. Select Skipped
ExpectedResult: Session status updated to Skipped

Scenario: Verify Version Status can be updated to Halted
Steps:
1. Open Version Status modal
2. Select Halted
ExpectedResult: Session status updated to Halted

Scenario: Verify Version Status modal shows module checklist before status selection
Steps: 1. Click Version Status button
ExpectedResult: Modal shows list of modules with tested/total counts

Scenario: Verify previous session versions are listed on Start Testing page
Steps: 1. Go to Start Testing page
ExpectedResult: All previous sessions listed with version status and date

Scenario: Verify status badge shows correct color per version status
Steps: 1. Check session list on Start Testing page
ExpectedResult: Completed = green Skipped = yellow Halted = red In Progress = blue

Scenario: Verify all modules are visible in the sidebar
Steps:
1. Start a session
2. Check sidebar
ExpectedResult: All modules listed with test case counts

Scenario: Verify clicking a module loads its test cases
Steps: 1. Click any module in sidebar
ExpectedResult: Test cases for that module display in main area

Scenario: Verify tester can mark a test case as Pass
Steps: 1. Click Pass on a test case
ExpectedResult: Case marked Pass — count updates in header

Scenario: Verify tester can mark a test case as Fail
Steps: 1. Click Fail on a test case
ExpectedResult: Case marked Fail — count updates in header

Scenario: Verify tester can mark a test case as Blocked
Steps: 1. Click Blocked on a test case
ExpectedResult: Case marked Blocked — count updates in header

Scenario: Verify result is saved automatically without page reload
Steps:
1. Mark a test case result
2. Switch to another module and return
ExpectedResult: Previous result still saved

Scenario: Verify tester can add a comment to a test case
Steps:
1. Click comment icon on a test case
2. Enter comment
3. Save
ExpectedResult: Comment saved and visible on the test case

Scenario: Verify tester can attach a screenshot or file to a test case
Steps:
1. Click attachment icon
2. Upload a file
ExpectedResult: File attached — visible on the test case

Scenario: Verify progress bar updates as test cases are marked
Steps:
1. Mark several test cases
2. Check module progress bar
ExpectedResult: Bar fills proportionally to tested cases

Scenario: Verify tester can filter test cases by status (Pass/Fail/Blocked/Untested)
Steps:
1. Use filter dropdown in test runner
2. Select a status
ExpectedResult: Only test cases matching selected status shown

Scenario: Verify tester can search test cases by keyword
Steps: 1. Enter keyword in search box
ExpectedResult: Matching test cases shown — others hidden

Scenario: Verify filter resets when switching modules
Steps:
1. Apply filter in Module A
2. Switch to Module B
ExpectedResult: Filter cleared — all test cases shown in Module B

Scenario: Verify tester can filter by priority (High/Medium/Low)
Steps: 1. Use priority filter
ExpectedResult: Only matching priority test cases shown

Scenario: Verify tester can export test results to Excel
Steps:
1. Click Export in test runner
2. Select Excel
ExpectedResult: Excel file downloaded with all test results

Scenario: Verify tester can export test results to CSV
Steps:
1. Click Export
2. Select CSV
ExpectedResult: CSV file downloaded with all test results

Scenario: Verify export modal shows module checklist before exporting
Steps: 1. Click Export button
ExpectedResult: Modal shows list of modules to include/exclude

Scenario: Verify exported file contains correct Pass/Fail/Blocked data
Steps:
1. Export results
2. Open file
ExpectedResult: Data in file matches what was marked in test runner

Scenario: Verify exported file includes test case ID scenario steps and expected result
Steps: 1. Open exported Excel/CSV
ExpectedResult: All test case fields present in output

Scenario: Verify dashboard shows current session pass/fail/blocked counts
Steps: 1. Go to Dashboard during active session
ExpectedResult: Live counts shown for current session

Scenario: Verify dashboard module breakdown chart renders correctly
Steps: 1. Open Dashboard
ExpectedResult: Module chart shows distribution of results

Scenario: Verify dashboard shows percentage completion for current session
Steps: 1. Open Dashboard
ExpectedResult: Completion % shown based on tested/total cases

Scenario: Verify dashboard data updates after marking test cases
Steps:
1. Mark several cases
2. Reload Dashboard
ExpectedResult: Updated counts and charts shown

Scenario: Verify dashboard shows historical comparison across sessions
Steps:
1. Complete multiple sessions
2. Open Dashboard
ExpectedResult: Trend or comparison chart visible for past sessions
